    Global Search Dialog
      1. Our Vision
      2. Affirming Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

      Affirming Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

      RWJF is defending and advancing the fundamental American values of diversity, equity, and inclusion with resolve and urgency.

      In a moment of retrenchment and backsliding, we are moving forward with increased investments to diversify the health professions; supporting the legal, communications, and organizing efforts of partners working toward racial equity; and funding the preservation of critical health research, particularly data broken down by racial and ethnic subgroups. These actions will improve health for all people.

      Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion: Core to Our Mission 

      Diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) efforts create access and opportunity for people who have historically faced greater barriers to living their healthiest lives—barriers that we must dismantle to achieve a future where health is no longer a privilege, but a right. We are pursuing this future through three ambitious goals built on the promise of diversity, equity, and inclusion:

      • We advocate for a healthcare system that provides respectful, high-quality care for everyone and a Public Health System that works together with communities to dismantle structural racism as a root cause of health disparities in America.
      • We envision flourishing communities where everyone has access to clean, safe drinking water and safe, stable housing; where jobs pay a living wage; where we have torn down the walls of segregation and all residents receive the respect and dignity they deserve.
      • We seek to build an economy that invests in families and values people for more than what they contribute to economic production; where all forms of care are accessible and affordable for families, and caregivers are valued for the critical role they play in our society.

       

      Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Strengthens Democracy

      Racial equity is key to a stable and thriving democracy where everyone feels a sense of belonging and inclusion.  The forces that sow racial inequality divide people and weaken community bonds, opening the door to authoritarianism.  

       

      How We Practice Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

      Diversity, equity, and inclusion is fundamental to everything we do and everything we seek to achieve. Within our organization, we are examining our past to learn lessons that can guide our ability to advance intersectional racial equity and justice. We are building an organizational culture of belonging for our employees. We are codifying systems, policies, practices, and partnerships that will help to hold us accountable to our diversity, equity, and inclusion commitments. And most importantly, we are explicitly addressing structural racism and other inequities through our grantmaking and investments.

       

      Reaffirming Our Shared Vision

      “RWJF supports thousands of organizations and networks working to advance our generational goals—dozens of whom are specifically focused on the emerging and sustained legal threats to racial diversity, equity, and inclusion.”

      —Richard Besser, MD, RWJF President and CEO

      Read the message

      Promoting Policy Tools That Advance Health and Racial Equity

      RWJF is committed to promoting and protecting inclusive policymaking and ballot measure access—two  tools of democracy that are critical to achieving a world where health is not a privilege for some but a right for all.

      Learn more

      5 Ways Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Drives Our Work

      • Using imagination as a tool for liberation 
      • Advancing cross-movement solidarity 
      • Expanding representation in healthcare
      • Spreading lessons for advancing equity in challenging times
      • Supporting race-conscious policies
      • Advancing DEI in higher education
      • Diversifying the health professions
      • Confronting racist legacies
      • Condemning racial violence

       

      • Building local alignment
      • Supporting community-led evaluation

      • Advancing digital accessibility and workplace inclusion
      • Supporting inclusive data-collection
      • Challenging ableism anti-vaccine narratives
      • Supporting tools that empower people with disabilities to have a say in policies
      • Engaging in ongoing dialogue with the communities we serve 

      • Participating in a truth, repair and transformation process
      • Embracing partnerships to hold ourselves accountable
      • Listening to and collaborating with the communities we serve 

      •  Shaping federal policy
      • Protecting race-conscious policies
      • Advancing reproductive justice
      • Transforming policies in our home state of New Jersey

      Related Content

      Why Diversifying the Health Professions Matters for Everyone

      We are committed to fostering a diverse workforce so every person, regardless of who they are or where they come from, can access the care they deserve to live a healthier life.

      Read the blog post

      Ramsey County, Minnesota

      The Board of Commissioners of this 2023 RWJF Culture of Health Prize-winning community passed a resolution that unanimously declared that “racism is a public health crisis” soon after George Floyd was murdered in Minneapolis.

      Read the grantee story
      Blog Post

      Evaluation that Builds Community Power Begins with Trust

      Equitable evaluation uplifts and empowers historically undervalued or unheard perspectives which advances health and racial equity.
      Health Disparities Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Community Power
      Blog Post

      Community-Led Research is No Longer a “Nice-to-Have”

      When communities lead research, their local knowledge informs powerful solutions for advancing health and racial equity. In this spirit, we are developing the Health Equity Research for Action program.
      Health Disparities Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
      Blog Post

      The Community Investment Philanthropy is Overlooking

      Innovative approaches to advancing health and racial equity have taken root here, including efforts to reimagine data systems. Sustained funding will strengthen and scale impact.
      Equitable Community Development Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Infrastructure & Energy
      View all

      Stay informed

      Sign up for the latest funding alerts, news, research, and perspectives from RWJF.

      Email address already subscribed

      Email address already subscribed. Please check your inbox to manage your subscriptions.

      Subscribed!

      Thank you. You are now subscribed.

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive. Select at least one (required).

      Be informed with our twice a month newsletter updating you with relevant news and research around a Culture of Health, as well as the latest funding opportunities.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs many robust health policy debates on Capitol Hill, covering topics like health equity, improving access to quality healthcare, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      Sometimes we have news, announcements or opportunities that don't quite fit the subscription parameters above. If you're interested, we'll send you this information under "There's more...". *If you've indicated you are an EU resident, we will only send these communications if you intentionally check this box.
